His Language : Like Kdliddsa our author wields language with
rare felicity as well as facility. His language is remarkable for its
simplicity, lucidity, delightful diction and lilting alliteration. Though
he takes poetic licence many a time in respect of grammatical con-
struction, gender, etc., his style is distinguished by what are called
Hrdkfdpdka, Prasdda, Mddhurya, Sukumdratd and other poetic excel-
lences. Where necessary he uses Ojas i.e. long compounds, also for
effectiveness as in the description of Agastya (XII).
